headline: Private Hospital Charges Rise 35% in 3 Years With No Price Controls
context: Average private hospital charges increased 35% between 2023 and 2025 as the sector remains largely unregulated on pricing.

cards.big: Private hospital charges rose 35% in 3 years with average bill now RM12,500 per admission
cards.sub: No government price controls exist for private hospital services in Malaysia

cards.big: Private Healthcare Facilities and Services Act 1998 enables fee regulation but power was never exercised
cards.sub: Section 57 fee schedule has been in draft form for over 20 years

cards.big: Insurance premiums increased 18% in 2025 partly driven by rising private hospital costs
cards.sub: Healthcare cost inflation feeds back into insurance affordability for consumers

cards.big: Hong Kong publishes mandatory private hospital fee transparency with itemized procedure costs
cards.sub: Price transparency allows patients to compare and choose based on published rates

cards.big: An industry that can raise prices 35% without oversight is not a free market—it is an unregulated monopoly
Should Malaysia finally implement the fee schedule provisions of the 1998 healthcare act
